name: Braised Pork Shoulder with Plums and Figs

ingredients.item: chicken broth, plus more as needed
In a large bowl, toss the pork with 1 tablespoon of olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
In a 7- to 9-quart Dutch oven over medium-high heat, heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Working in batches, add the pork and cook for 5 to 7 minutes per side to brown. Transfer the pork to a bowl and set aside. Drain the fat from the pot and return it to medium heat.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil to the pot along with the onions. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, until translucent.
Add 1 pound of plums and cook for 1 or 2 minutes.
Stir in the wine, vinegar, and honey. Cook for about 5 minutes, until reduced by half.
Return the pork to the pot.
Add the bay leaves, the ground nutmeg, and enough chicken broth to cover the pork. Cover the pot and place it in the oven. Braise for 90 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and place it on the stove top. with a slotted spoon, remove the pork from the pot into a serving dish.
Add the remaining ½ pound of plums and the figs to the pot. Cook uncovered over medium heat for about 8 minutes to reduce the sauce just a bit.
Add the reduced sauce along with the plums and figs to the pork in the serving dish, toss together, and enjoy.
